cards.back: A widely used optimization technique for training artificial neural networks, which involves propagating the error gradients backwards through the network
A method for computing the gradients of a function with respect to its variables, without explicitly differentiating the function
The partial derivatives of the quadratic cost function with respect to each weight, used to update the weights during backpropagation
The process of iteratively updating the weights of a neural network by moving in the opposite direction of the gradients of the cost function, to minimize the loss
A first-order optimization algorithm that updates the weights of a neural network by descending the gradient of the cost function, to minimize the loss
